title: Level 259

[[File:{{{file|259.png}}}|{{{bg-size|500px}}}]]
'''Level 259''' is the inside of a subway train with a variable number of cars ranging from a minimum of two to an infinite number of cars. The train will have an average of nine cars. During the commute, the number of cars in the train may change at random. The number of cars increases or decreases without affecting the train's speed. The train maintains a maximum speed of 55 mph (~89 km/h). The car's interior comprises of the following components: *Six flip benches. ''Each bench contains eight seats and two standing rails. The flip component may be found between every two chairs and reveals a wheelchair access space. *6 windows positioned above flip benches. *6 digital panels are positioned above the windows. '''Pay attention to the information shown on these panels!''' *Three folding doors on each side of the car allow commuters to exit and enter. *1 sliding door in the front and back of the car allows access to the next car over. The interior of the cars contains an anomaly that allows huge beings, such as the [[Malveillant]], to board and exit the train and move between cars. There have been no reports of any [[Malveillant]] riding in these cars. There have been multiple reports of "Native Americans" riding on this train. The subway is primarily used by [[Facelings]], but other entities can also board it. More information can be found further in the page. '''Level 259''' requires a randomly selected human (one of six non-hostile humans) to survive their trip on this train. This individual will be referred to as the 'boarding passenger'. The boarding passenger must follow the rules below. Boarding passengers will be brutally stalked by hostile entities unless they make a 'friend' (a.k.a. obtain protection). Keep in mind that certain 'friends' provide more protection than others. The boarding passenger may potentially be exposed to a psychological threat. Everything will be discussed in full below. But in very rare cases, your friend might hate you and try to kill you. If you are in a bad situation, then your friend will most likely abandon you. {{User:TheBackroomsMapMaker/-229/Tabbers/Hazards}}
---- ----
'''Are you sure?''' ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎

'''Are you sure?'''‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎  
''This message will appear if the wanderer is potentially dangerous.'' There is a 0.05% probability that one of these five non-hostile wanderers will recieve combat training. It is strongly advised that you become friends with human wanderers who have combat training. Hostile entities and wanderers will try to separate you and your friend, but they risk being harmed or killed in the process because they are not permitted to harm them. It's worth noting that discovering human wanderers on this train is nearly impossible. The subway will add vehicles, giving you extra area to search. This subway is largely used by [[Facelings]]. If you are in danger and need a friend, remember to consult the panel. The panel will indicate how close you are to a 'friend'. Human wanderers cannot assist you if you have violated '''Rule 3'''. They will attack you if you have violated '''Rule 2'''. Do not rely only on the safety of other wanderers. Some entities cannot be stopped by another wanderer, and you place that other wanderer in a position to witness your death.

''This message will appear if the 'Boarding passenger' is dying somewhere alone in '''Level 259''' and the digital panel is keeping them company until they pass.'' Less than 5% of lost ''''Boarding Passengers'''' will find their way back. Less than 10% will try to no-clip and end up somewhere worse. ---- ----
=Entrances=
---- ---- ====Entrance: Pre-existing passenger ==== *These passengers will be randomly extracted from the human pool of explorers present in '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. ====Entrance: Boarding passenger ==== *There is a 70% chance a random person will be extracted from the human pool of explorers present in '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. *Slipping and falling off the platform and landing on the train tracks has a 50% change of you no-clipping and ending up in '''Level 259'''. *No-clipping on the train tracks in '''[[Level 913]]''' has a 10% change of you ending in '''Level 259'''. *Blocking the train door of '''[[Level 61]]''' has a 30% chance of you ending up in '''Level 259'''. Just do not block the doors in '''Level 259'''. *Jumping off the train in '''[[Level 307]]''' has a 25% chance of you ending up in '''Level 259'''. *Maybe, maybe if you're very lucky, we can give you a [[Level 179|bus]] to here. Noclip through the void during a quantum fluctuation, which in itself is an extremely rare, almost impossible occurrence. Maybe then you'll get a bus to '''[[Level 259]]'''. *A train stuck in the roof found in '''[[Level 994]]''' will bring one here once the wanderer(s) open the doors and enter. *Hearing train noises in '''[[Level 406]]''' may make ones' ears bleed and black out. They will wake up in '''[[Level 259]]'''. *An abandoned, dilapidated train found in '''[[Level 702]]''' may send one here. ---- ----
Amygdala's exit(s):''' He will help you get off at '''[[Level 138]]''' if you have been exposed to the psychological hazard. He will help you get off at '''[[Level 707]]''' if you have not been exposed. *'''Vitocoz's exit:''' He will help you get off in '''[[Level 11]]''' if you are not exposed. *'''Astrid's exit:''' He will help you get off at any safe level of your choosing. *'''Your exit:''' Watching the digital panel for your choice of exit will take you to that exit. *'''Exit: Bad endings''' **Failure to move from a car that is being jettisoned from the subway will result in you being 'lost'. **Going too deep into '''Level 259''' has a 60% chance of you getting lost and forced to wander the infinite cars of '''Level 259''' until you perish of starvation or thirst or attempt to no-clip to escape. **Attempting to no-clip to escape '''Level 259''' after getting 'lost' will result in you ending up in a random level with a survival classification of 5 or falling into '''[[The Void]]'''. **Being forced off the train because you violated rule 2 has a 75% chance of you falling into '''[[The Void]]''' and 25% of you ending up in '''[[Level 6]]'''. __NOEDITSECTION__ [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Infohazard]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] ---- ---- {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Original Author''': Silent Moderator [[User:MoonlightSerenity|MoonlightSerenity]]
